title: CXXRTL output doesn't compile with zero-sized ports on modules
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.32 (git sha1 fbab08acf14, clang++ 11.1.0 -fPIC -Os)

### On which OS did this happen?

macOS

### Reproduction Steps

Compiling a design with an instance of a module with a zero-sized port (as an edge case in the module's specialization)

### Expected Behavior

Compilation goes through

### Actual Behavior

Compilation of the CXXRTL output stumbles on
```
/include/backends/cxxrtl/cxxrtl.h:1095:3: error: static_assert failed due to requirement 'sizeof (item) == value<0>::chunks * sizeof(unsigned int)' "value<Bits> is not compatible with C layout"
                static_assert(sizeof(item) == value<Bits>::chunks * sizeof(chunk_t),
                ^             ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
```

I assume this is due to empty structs not having zero size in C++.
code_blocks:
/include/backends/cxxrtl/cxxrtl.h:1095:3: error: static_assert failed due to requirement 'sizeof (item) == value<0>::chunks * sizeof(unsigned int)' "value<Bits> is not compatible with C layout"
                static_assert(sizeof(item) == value<Bits>::chunks * sizeof(chunk_t),
                ^             ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
